lines.text: Central Perk, the gang, except Rachel, is watching a new singer.

lines.speaker: Singer
lines.text: (singing) 'Cause every time I see your face, I can't help but fall from grace. I know.....

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! This girl is good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh-ho yeah! A song with rhyming words. Oo, I never thought of that before.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I like her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Chandler) Why? Because she can sing and play guitar and do both at the same time?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that's pretty much all I'm looking for from these people.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Look at you. All jealous.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah Pheebs, come on, you two have completely different styles. Y'know, she's more..(shakes his shoulders, like he's dancing) y'know, and you're more (sees the look from Phoebe and stops)

lines.speaker: Singer
lines.text: (singing) beside meeeee-eeee-ee. (everyone applauds her).

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, see, see, everyone else is happy she's done.

lines.speaker: Singer
lines.text: Okay, my next song's called: Phoebe Buffay, What Can I Say. I Really Loved When We Were Singing Partners, And I Shouldn't Have Left You That Way.
The gang all looks at Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, one of those 'look for the hidden meaning' songs.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, later.

lines.speaker: Singer
lines.text: Hey Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Leslie, how'd you know I'd be here?

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: I ran into Vlad at the place where they sell the big fish, and he said you played here a lot, so umm....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross) All right listen, I have to go to the bathroom, but if the place with the big fish comes up again. I'd like know whether that's several big fish or just one big fish.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Leslie) So ah, Phoebe tells us you write jingles.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Actually I said she abandoned me to write jingles.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Leslie) Ah, anything we might of heard of?

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Ah, yeah, umm. (singing) Home is never far away..

lines.speaker: Monica, Ross, and Joey
lines.text: (joining in) Home is Home Star stew.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Yeah, but, I don't do that anymore. I got kinda sick of it, and then I couldn't come up with anything good, so they fired me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmm, bummer.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Well, I y'know, I was just, umm, I was just thinking and hoping, that umm, maybe you'd want to get back together?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. But thanks.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Aw come on Phoebe would you just think about it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. No. But thanks.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Okay, ah, see ya Pheebs. (leaves)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Wow, that was kinda brutal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well okay, let this be a lesson to all of you, all right. Once you, once you betray me, I become like the ice woman, y'know. Very cold, hard, unyielding, y'know nothing, nothing can penetrate this icy exterior. (to Monica) Can I have a tissue, please?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, sure.
Outside the bathroom, Chandler is pacing back and fourth, waiting is use it.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (to Chandler) Someone in there?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. This is just part of a dare devil game that I play called 'wait until the last moment before I burst and die.'
The door opens.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Jeez, man did you fall..(sees it's a beautiful woman coming out of the men's room) Hi! So ah, did ya, did-did-did ya fall high?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Someone was in the lady's room, I couldn't wait. I left the lid up for ya though.
Gunther walks up

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Gunther) Y'know what Gunther, go ah, go ahead, I'm-I'm talking to ah, (tries to get her to say her name). (to her) This is the part where you say your name.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Ginger.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ginger. I'm talking to Ginger, so....

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Don't you have to use the bathroom?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope, nope, I'd just ah, I'd rather talk to you. (pause) Yes, I do. Yes, I do have to go to the bathroom. (knocks on the door)

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Someone in here.
Central Perk, the gang's putting their coats on to leave.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where's Chandler?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, he can't make it, he said he had to his... (sees Ginger) Whoa-oh! (hides behind the coat rack.)

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Joey? Joey Tribbiani?
She walks over behind the coat rack, but Joey picks it up and moves it so that he's still behind it, and she can't see him.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Joey I can see you okay? You're hiding behind the coats.
Joey puts his finger over his mouth to tell Ross to keep quiet. Ginger looks at Monica who looks away and leave.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phew, close one.
Rachel's office, Mark is packing his stuff into a box.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, sweetie!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Hi, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I've got some bad news.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can get a quick bite to eat, but then I have to come back up here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on sweetie! You've had to work late every night for the past two weeks, what is it this time?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Actually, it's kinda my fault. I-I quit today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) But work comes first! (to Mark) Oh hey, but that's sad about you though, what happened? Burn out? Burn all out, did ya?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nooo, he's leaving for a better job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh well that's great, so I guess this is ah, this is good bye then. Huh? (picks a pad up off Rachel's desk and tosses it into his box) Good bye.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Okay, then.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well we're gonna miss you around here.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Yeah, me too.
Rachel goes to hug him but Ross is holding one of her hands and doesn't let go, so she can only put one arm around him.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: So, see ya on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you bet.
Ross is shocked, but Rachel drags him out of the office.
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe and Monica are playing with a Ouija board, Phoebe's beeper goes off and Monica screams.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. (takes her beeper puts in a pot, covers it, and puts the pot in the oven)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know those are a delicacy in India.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, that was Leslie calling again to see if we can get back together. That's the twentieth time today! And good luck Leslie!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, she must have hurt you pretty bad, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, yeah. Y'know, we were best friends, ever since we were little, our Moms worked on the barge together.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you two must have been so cute running around on a barge.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You never run on a barge!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is ah, is Chandler around?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, umm, he met some girl at the coffee house.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oooh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, Ginger something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nooo. No, no, ah, are you sure it wasn't something that sounded like Ginger, like ah, Gingeer?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it was Ginger. I remember, because when he told me, I said, (singing) 'the movie star.'

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aww, man. That's the girl I was hiding from. When she finds out he's my roommate, she's gonna tell him what I did.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what did you do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What, oh, oh, oh, no, no, I can't, I can't tell you that, it's like the most awful, horrible thing I've ever done my whole life.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what, don't tell us. We'll just wait until Chandler gets home, (to Phoebe) because it'll be more fun that way.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right!! Okay, it was like four years ago. Okay, Ginger and I had gone out a few times, and then this one weekend, we went up to her Dad's cabin. Just me, her, and her annoying little dog Pepper. Well that night, I cooked this really romantic dinner....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You gave her food poisoning!?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I wish. No. After dinner, me, her, and Pepper all fell asleep in front of the fire. Well I woke up in the middle of the night and I saw that the fire was dying out. So, I picked up a log and threw it on. Or, at least what I thought was a log.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!! You threw Pepper on the fire!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I wish. See, I guess another thing I probably should've told you about Ginger is that she kinda has a ah, artificial leg.
Phoebe and Monica both stand up and gasp.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Joey, what did you do after you threw her leg on the fire?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I ran!!
A street, Chandler is kissing Ginger.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that's the best kiss I've had with anyone I've ever met in a men's room.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Actually, me too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sees her foot is in a slush puddle) Op, foot in a puddle, foot all in a puddle.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Oh damn, I hate that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we're gonna have to get you out of those shoes.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Oh, don't worry about it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, really you're gonna freeze.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: No, I'm not.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're not, what do you, what do you got a bionic foot?

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Some day, maybe.
Ross's bedroom, Rachel getting into bed while Ross is reading and laughs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Funny book?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmmm. Oh, no, no, I just thinking about something funny I heard today. Umm, Mark, Mark saying 'I'll see you Saturday.'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, at the lecture, I told you that last week, you said you didn't mind.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no, no, no, it's-it's not the lecture ah, I mind, umm....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, please tell me it's not because I'm going with Mark.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, well...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!!! Ross!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I'm sorry, but ah, look if you're not working with him anymore, why do you have to still do stuff with him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because, he's my friend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, but do you really need another friend? I mean...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well if I stop playing with Joey and Chandler, can I play with Mark?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is that funny? Am I supposed to be laughing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, you thought 'See you Saturday' was funny. Look honey, Mark is in fashion okay, I like having a friend that I can share this stuff with. You guys would never want to go to a lecture with me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pa-haa!! I would love to go with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really!?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, hey I-I have clothes, I even pick them out. I mean for, for all you know I could be a fashion..... monger.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Honey, I would love for you to go with me. (Ross has a worried look on his face) What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What should I wear, now I'm all nervous.
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is looking at her beeper still in the pot. She takes it out, shakes it, and puts it back in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know they say a watched pot never beeps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's just y'know, been a couple of hours, and she hasn't called. Not that I even care, y'know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe why don't you just call her? You obviously want to.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You think you know me so well.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, don't 'cha wanna?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, well I do know you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's what I said.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well so?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't. I can't. She dumped me, I mean I totally trusted her and then one day it was 'Okay, bye Pheebs' gone. Y'know what the saddest part is, when we were playing together, that was like the most fun I've ever had in like all my lives.
Central Perk, Leslie is singing.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: (singing) My best shoes, so good to me. I wear them everyday. Down at the heel, holes in the toes. Don't care what people say. My feet's best friends, pals to the end. With them I'm one hot chicky. Though late one night, not much light, I....
Phoebe runs in and joins her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) I stepped in something icky.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Leslie
lines.text: (singing) Sticky shoes, sticky shoes, always make me smile. Sticky shoes, sticky shoes, next time I'll.... avoid the..... pillleeeee.
Commercial Break
The lecture, Rachel is listening closely, Ross is bored out of his mind.

lines.speaker: Lecturer
lines.text: We're beginning to see a lot of layering of sheer fabrics and colours. For instance a sheer navy blouse over a pink....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) I'm really glad we came. (Rachel smiles and rubs his arm) You're so pretty. I love you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. (puts her hand over his mouth)
Central Perk, Phoebe is teaching Leslie how to sing Smelly Cat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) Smelly cat, smelly cat, what are they feeding you? Smelly cat, smelly cat, it's not your fault.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Wow, that's great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah!

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Y'know you could totally sell this. It'd be perfect for like umm, a kitty litter campaign.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I..., a jingle? No, no-no-no, no.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: What? Why not? You could make a ton of money.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well if I was in this for the money, I'd be a millionaire by now, y'know. You just got to get out of that jingle head sweetie.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Aw, you're right, you're right. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's okay. All right, I'm gonna play song that's really, really sad. It's called Magician Box Mix Up. (she turns her guitar upside down to play it.)
The lecture, Ross is passed out against Rachel's shoulder.

lines.speaker: Lecturer
lines.text: ....oversized bracelets, oversized earrings, oversizing of accessories in general are very popular now.
Ross wakes up with a start and startles Rachel. The guy next to him starts laughing, which starts Ross laughing, Rachel gives him a look and he stops.
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is reading the newspaper.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, can I borrow this? (points to his milk) My milk's gone bad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I hate that. I once had a thing of half and half, stole my car.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So umm, how was your date with Ginger?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great. It was great. She's ah, she's great, great looking, great personality, she's greatness.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sounds like she's got the ah, whole package.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey told you about the leg, huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh God, it freaked me out. Okay, I know it shouldn't have, but it did. I mean I like her, I don't want to stop seeing her, but every so often it's like 'Hey, y'know what, where's your leg?' I mean I'm the smallest person in the world aren't I? I'm the smallest person in the world.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering from his bedroom) Morning.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Monica) Actually he's the smallest person in the world.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Chandler) Heard about the leg burnin' huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It came up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen, I ah, I know it's a longshot. But, by any chance did she find that funny?
Both Chandler and Monica walk away in disgust.
Monica and Rachel's, Ross and Rachel are getting back from the lecture.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) So I nodded off a little.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nodded off!! Ross you were snoring. My father's boat didn't make that much noise when it hit rocks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! Forty-five minutes! Forty-five minutes the man talked about strappy backed dresses.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well okay, how about four hours in a freezing museum auditorium listening to Professor Pitstains and he's 'Hey everybody! Remember that thing that's been dead for a gazillion years. Well there's this little bone we didn't know it had!'

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: First of all it's Professor Pittain! And second of all, that little bone, proved that, that particular dinosaur had wings, but didn't fly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, see now, what I just heard: blah-blah-blah, blah-blah-blah-blah-blah, blah-blah-blah, blah, blah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what, 100 million people went to see a movie about what I do, I wonder how many people would go see a movie called, Jurassic Parka.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that is so...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no-no, a bunch of out of control jackets take over an island. (Makes an unusual sound, then he realises that he still has his jacket on and quickly tries to shake it off, thinking it's alive and attacking him.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know if what I do is so lame, then why did you insist on coming with me this morning? Huh? Was it so I just wouldn't go with Mark?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. I... I wanted to be with you. I don't know, I feel like lately, I feel like you're slipping away from me, y'know. With this new job, and all these new people, and you've got this whole other life going on. I-I-I know it's dumb, but I hate that I'm not a part of it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not dumb. But, maybe it's okay that you're not a part of it. Y'know what I mean? (Ross looks confused) I mean it's like, I-I-I like that you're not involved in that part of my life.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's a little clearer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey see, it doesn't mean that I don't love you. Because I do. I love you, I love you so much. But my work it's-it's for me y'know, I'm out there, on my own, and I'm doing it and it's scary but I love it, because it's mine. I, but, I mean is that okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, I-I-I... (hugs her and mouths No!!)
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler and Ginger are eating dinner.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Your thinking about my leg aren't you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. No. Actually I forgot, what is the deal with that again?

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: It's okay if it bothers you. Really. I mean the only thing I need to know is: 'How much it bothers you?' because I don't like wasting my time. Am I wasting my time?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. No. I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Okay. It's just like anything else, you just have to get used to it.
They start making out. She opens his shirt and feels inside and stops.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's-that's my nubbin.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: What's a nubbin?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's kinda a ah, a third nipple kinda thing.

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: You have three nipples?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, y'know two regulars. And ah one that barely qualifies as... (starts to kiss her again, but she gets up.) Ahh, what?

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Nothing. I, I just remembered I have to leave.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You ah, you have, you have to leave, now? How come?

lines.speaker: Ginger
lines.text: Ah well, it's nubbin. Nothing! Umm. Y'know what, I'll see you later. Okay. (She leaves and in the hall we see her shake her shoulders like when someone runs their fingernails across a blackboard.)
Central Perk, Phoebe is getting ready to sing as Leslie enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Leslie) Oh, I thought you weren't coming. What? Where were you?

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: Come here, come here. (they go to the side of the stage) Okay, don't get mad, okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, don't give me a reason to get mad, okay

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: I played Smelly Cat for the people at my old ad agency, they went nuts.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, look, I told you that I didn't want you to try and sell it, and you just, you big fat did it anyway. God, y'know what, I think five years ago I probably would've done anything to play with you but, I can do it by myself. And if I can't trust you then just forget it.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.text: No, no, I don't want to forget it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay y'know what you have to choose. All right, if-if the most important thing on the planet to you is this cat poopy thing then, okay you can have Smelly Cat, but we won't be partners. So what's it gonna be?
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe, Monica, and Joey are watching TV.

lines.speaker: Commercial
lines.text: (in the background their singing Smelly Cat) Problem odour in the litter box? Don't change your kitty, change your kitty litter.
Monica gets up and shuts off the TV.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sorry, Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. You okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. I actually am, yeah. Y'know life-life's gonna had you all kinds of stuff, y'know you learn your little lessons and hopefully you grow. Wanna hear a new song?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We'd love too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (singing) 'Jingle bitch screwed me over! Go to hell jingle whore! Go to hell Go to hell. Go to hell-hell-hell.' That's all I have so far.
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, the whole gang except Chandler is there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering, happily) Well hello!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where have you been?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The doctor.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes! Just had me a little nubbin-ectomy. Yep! Two nipples, no waiting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! It's like Rachel in High School.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! Come on, I was kidding! It was such an obvious joke!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was an obvious joke, and I didn't think of it. Why didn't I think of it? The source of all my powers. Oh dear God, what have I done!
End
